title: Shakespearean Insults Reference
description: A reference collection of insults drawn from the works of William Shakespeare. Each entry pairs an authentic (or authentic-style) quotation with its source play, the character who delivers it where known, and the act and scene. Intended as a reusable reference for anyone in want of a more eloquent affront.

fields.insult: The insulting line as spoken in the text.
fields.source: The play the line is drawn from.
fields.speaker: The character who delivers the insult, where identifiable.
fields.act_scene: Act and scene of the source play, where identifiable.

insults.insult: Thou art a boil, a plague sore, an embossed carbuncle in my corrupted blood.
insults.speaker: Lear

insults.insult: Away, you three-inch fool!
insults.speaker: Curtis

insults.insult: Thou clay-brained guts, thou knotty-pated fool, thou whoreson obscene greasy tallow-catch!
insults.speaker: Prince Hal

insults.insult: I'll beat thee, but I would infect my hands.
insults.speaker: Timon

insults.insult: Thou art as fat as butter.
insults.speaker: Prince Hal

insults.insult: Would thou wert clean enough to spit upon!
insults.speaker: Timon

insults.insult: You are not worth the dust which the rude wind blows in your face.
insults.speaker: Rosalind

insults.insult: Thou art a very ragged wart.
insults.speaker: Falstaff

insults.insult: More of your conversation would infect my brain.
insults.speaker: Menenius

insults.insult: Thine face is not worth sunburning.
insults.speaker: King Henry

insults.insult: He has not so much brain as ear-wax.
insults.speaker: Thersites

insults.insult: Thou art unfit for any place but hell.
insults.speaker: Lady Anne

insults.insult: Out of my sight! Thou dost infect mine eyes.
insults.speaker: Lady Anne

insults.insult: Methink'st thou art a general offence and every man should beat thee.
insults.speaker: Lafew

insults.insult: Thou whoreson zed! Thou unnecessary letter!
insults.speaker: Kent

insults.insult: Thou art the cap of all the fools alive.
insults.speaker: Apemantus

insults.insult: You scullion! You rampallian! You fustilarian! I'll tickle your catastrophe!
insults.speaker: Falstaff

insults.insult: Thou cream-faced loon! Where got'st thou that goose look?
insults.speaker: Macbeth

insults.insult: There's no more faith in thee than in a stewed prune.
insults.speaker: Falstaff

insults.insult: Peace, ye fat-guts!
insults.speaker: Prince Hal

insults.insult: Thou art a flesh-monger, a fool and a coward.
insults.speaker: Lucio

insults.insult: Thou sodden-witted lord! Thou hast no more brain than I have in mine elbows.
insults.speaker: Thersites

insults.insult: I do desire we may be better strangers.
insults.speaker: Orlando

insults.insult: Thou art like a toad; ugly and venomous.
insults.speaker: Duke Senior

insults.insult: His wit's as thick as Tewkesbury mustard.
insults.speaker: Falstaff

insults.insult: The tartness of his face sours ripe grapes.
insults.speaker: Menenius

insults.insult: Thou art a very dull fool; only the gift is good in nothing but in beauty.
insults.speaker: Olivia

insults.insult: You blocks, you stones, you worse than senseless things!
insults.speaker: Marullus

insults.insult: Thou art a strange fellow: a tailor make a man?
insults.speaker: Kent

insults.insult: Go, prick thy face, and over-red thy fear, thou lily-liver'd boy.
insults.speaker: Macbeth

insults.insult: Villain, I have done thy mother.
insults.speaker: Aaron

insults.insult: Thou art as loathsome as a toad.
insults.speaker: Cressida

insults.insult: A most notable coward, an infinite and endless liar, an hourly promise-breaker.
insults.speaker: First Lord

insults.insult: I am sick when I do look on thee.
insults.speaker: Demetrius

insults.insult: Get thee gone, and dig my grave thyself.
insults.speaker: King Henry

insults.insult: Thou art a knave, a rascal, an eater of broken meats; a base, proud, shallow, beggarly, three-suited, hundred-pound, filthy, worsted-stocking knave.
insults.speaker: Kent
